symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic (>3yr) lower back and bilateral hip pain, disproportionate to a minor original injury

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Long duration and disproportion to injury are hallmarks of central sensitisation / chronic pain syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute flare of pain after minor exertion, no new trauma

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A disproportionate flare to a minor trigger fits central sensitisation rather than new structural injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute total loss of mobility (cannot rise from chair or reach bathroom) in a person living alone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute immobility in an isolated elder is an independent trigger for urgent in-person assessment (falls, dehydration, incontinence).

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal bowel and bladder control, no saddle anaesthesia, no sudden leg weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of sphincter dysfunction, saddle anaesthesia and leg weakness makes cauda equina very unlikely on history.
Absence of fever/systemic signs argues against spinal infection, though does not fully exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sudden traumatic event and no acute height loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of trauma lowers but does not exclude osteoporotic fracture, which can occur with minimal force.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse lumbar paraspinal and bilateral gluteal tenderness WITHOUT focal bony point tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse soft-tissue tenderness without bony point tenderness fits a flare rather than a fracture — but requires palpation the AI cannot do.
Absence of focal bony point tenderness argues against fracture; sealed clinician exam finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact lower-limb neurology — normal sensation, normal reflexes, 5/5 motor power

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal neurological exam excludes acute radiculopathy / cord or cauda equina compression — a clinician measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Globally restricted range of motion due to guarding and apprehension, not mechanical block

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fear-avoidance guarding rather than fixed mechanical restriction supports a pain flare; requires in-person assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe insomnia and non-restorative sleep with profound fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sleep disturbance is part of the chronic-pain / central-sensitisation cluster and amplifies the flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prominent low mood, tearfulness, anxiety and irritability; social/physical withdrawal

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chronic pain syndrome with mood disturbance
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mood disturbance and withdrawal are core to the chronic-pain distress cycle; also mandates a suicide-risk screen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals — BP 145/85, HR 90, Temp 36.6, RR 18, SpO2 98%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mildly elevated BP/HR consistent with pain and distress; afebrile and normally saturated argue against sepsis. Clinician-measured — the AI cannot obtain these without a home device.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to definitively exclude cauda equina / cord compression — cannot be performed via telehealth, hence the urgent in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person spinal palpation for focal bony point tenderness
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Helps exclude an osteoporotic vertebral compression fracture; the AI cannot palpate.

Not first-line for a chronic pain flare with a reassuring history screen; reserved for the in-person clinician's judgement if fracture is suspected on examination. The AI Doctor should NOT reflexively order imaging.
Only if the in-person assessment raises concern for infection; afebrile with no systemic signs, so not urgently indicated.
